title: Mixed Graph: The bar chart below shows how tourism to Australia from three different countrie...
description: The bar chart below shows how tourism to Australia from three different countries changed between 1995 and 2005. The line chart illustrates
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Bar chart \+ Line graph: Tourists & Types of stay (1995–2005)

1. Giải thích đề

Biểu đồ cột: số lượng khách du lịch đến Australia từ UK, USA, Japan (1995 vs 2005).

Biểu đồ đường: số người chọn các loại hình lưu trú (hotel vs backpacking) từ 1995–2005.

→ Dạng: mixed (bar \+ line, change over time \+ comparison)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ:

number of tourists

types of stay

UK / USA / Japan

staying at hotels / backpacking

Mẫu:

The bar chart compares the number of tourists from the UK, the USA and Japan visiting Australia in 1995 and 2005, while the line graph illustrates changes in types of accommodation over the same period.

3. Overview (ý chính)

Lượng khách từ UK và USA tăng mạnh, Japan giảm

UK và USA đạt mức cao tương đương vào 2005 (\~1.5 million)

Japan là nước duy nhất giảm

Khách ở khách sạn tăng rồi giảm, trong khi backpacking tăng liên tục

Đến 2005, hai loại hình gần như ngang nhau

4. BODY 1 – Bar chart (tourists)

UK:

1995: \~800,000

2005: \~1,500,000 (tăng mạnh)

USA:

1995: \~500,000

2005: \~1,500,000 (tăng mạnh nhất)

Japan:

1995: \~1,450,000

2005: \~1,100,000 (giảm)

→ Ý chính:

  • USA có mức tăng lớn nhất
  • Japan chuyển từ cao nhất xuống thấp hơn

5. BODY 2 – Line graph (types of stay)

Staying at hotels:

1995: \~1.7 million

2000: \~2.4 million (đỉnh)

2005: \~1.2 million (giảm mạnh)

Backpacking:

1995: \~200,000

2000: \~700,000

2005: \~1.2 million (tăng đều)

→ Ý chính:

  • Xu hướng trái ngược
  • Backpacking bắt kịp hotel vào 2005

6. Gợi ý từ vựng

increase dramatically

remain stable / reach a peak

decline significantly

show an upward trend

overtake / catch up with

be comparable

The bar chart compares the number of tourists from the UK, the USA and Japan who visited Australia in 1995 and 2005, while the line graph shows changes in accommodation types over the same period.

Overall, visitor numbers from the UK and the USA increased significantly over the decade, whereas the figure for Japan declined. In terms of accommodation, staying in hotels was more popular initially, but backpacking grew steadily and matched hotel stays by 2005.

Looking first at tourist numbers, arrivals from the UK rose markedly from about 800,000 in 1995 to around 1.5 million in 2005. A similar but more dramatic increase was seen for visitors from the USA, climbing from roughly 500,000 to approximately 1.5 million. In contrast, Japanese tourism decreased from about 1.45 million in 1995 to just over 1.1 million in 2005.

Turning to accommodation, hotel stays increased from approximately 1.7 million in 1995 to a peak of around 2.4 million in 2000, before dropping sharply to about 1.2 million in 2005. By comparison, backpacking rose consistently throughout the period, starting at around 200,000 in 1995, increasing to roughly 700,000 in 2000, and reaching about 1.2 million in 2005, equal to hotel stays at the end of the period.
